scope.summary: Implement internal-line authoring on Panel geometry: add named internal edge records (InternalLine type with role: FoldLine | PleatLine | DartLine | StressSeam | Baseline) to GarmentSpec panels, distinct from the outer outline and seam edges, carried into UV/texture and solver constraint generation
acceptance_criteria: InternalLine type carries: id, panel_id, role (FoldLine | PleatLine | DartLine | StressSeam | Baseline | TopstichLine), start_cm: [f32;2], end_cm: [f32;2] or Bezier shape
add_internal_line(panel_id, role, geometry) stores the line in tailor_panels spec_json and emits TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded
Internal lines with role=FoldLine generate a fold seam constraint in the solver (distinct from seam join constraints), controlling fold angle
Internal lines with role=TopstitchLine are carried into the UV/texture renderer (MT-226 topstitch rendering feeds from these)
Internal lines do not affect PANEL_CLOSED (they are interior, not outline edges)
Model-accessible via edit_panel MCP tool with operation=add_internal_line
scope.risk_if_missed: MT-132 covers guide lines for topstitch/texture rendering only. Without a general InternalLine authoring API, fold lines (MD Fold Seam Line), dart lines with tip convergence, pleat fold indicators, and stress-seam lines cannot be authored as named, typed geometry. Dart handling (MT-122) and pleat handling (MT-123) depend on this substrate — without it they operate on implicit coordinates rather than authorable line records, making model-driven editing of darts and pleats fragile.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
